type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Our CLAIMs crossed; you take ownership of this file and implement both features. For my feature (throw error if resolve methods yield empty bytes): In each of ImageBlock.resolve_image, AudioBlock.resolve_audio, and DocumentBlock.resolve_document (llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py), after obtaining the BytesIO buffer from resolve_binary(...), check its size before returning: seek to end via buffer.seek(0, 2), get size via buffer.tell(), then buffer.seek(0) to reset position back to start. If size == 0, raise ValueError with message 'resolve_image returned zero bytes' (for resolve_image), 'resolve_audio returned zero bytes' (for resolve_audio), 'resolve_document returned zero bytes' (for resolve_document) - i.e. f'resolve_{type} returned zero bytes' using the method's type name. Only raise after confirming zero size; otherwise return the buffer positioned at the start unchanged. Apply this check as the last step before the return statement in each of the three methods, wrapping the existing resolve_binary(...) call result in a local variable first (e.g. buffer = resolve_binary(...); then the size check; then return buffer).
note: Deferring ownership to you since your CLAIM landed alongside mine; implement your resolve_*_with_size() work plus this spec

type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add three new methods that return tuple[BytesIO, int] (buffer, byte size):

1. In ImageBlock class, after resolve_image(): 
def resolve_image_with_size(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
    """Resolve an image such that PIL can read it, and also return its size in bytes.

    Args:
        as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
    """
    buffer = self.resolve_image(as_base64=as_base64)
    size = len(buffer.getvalue())
    return buffer, size

2. In AudioBlock class, after resolve_audio():
def resolve_audio_with_size(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
    """Resolve an audio such that PIL can read it, and also return its size in bytes.

    Args:
        as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved audio should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
    """
    buffer = self.resolve_audio(as_base64=as_base64)
    size = len(buffer.getvalue())
    return buffer, size

3. In DocumentBlock class, after resolve_document():
def resolve_document_with_size(self) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
    """Resolve a document such that it is represented by a BytesIO object, and also return its size in bytes."""
    buffer = self.resolve_document()
    size = len(buffer.getvalue())
    return buffer, size

Important: getvalue() does not consume/move the read position (unlike .read()), so the returned buffer remains fully seeked-to-start and usable by callers exactly like the original resolve_* methods. If you add your empty-buffer ValueError check inside resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document themselves, these wrapper methods will naturally inherit that validation for free - no special-casing needed on your end. Original resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document methods must remain unchanged in their existing signatures/behavior (only add your validation, not remove backward compat).
